metadata.summary: Using Figma AI tools (Make, Draw, Sites, AI Image Tools) for rapid prototype generation and static site publishing; agent pre/post-processing workflows.

content.title: Figma AI Ecosystem
content.summary: Figma AI tools (Config 2025+) compress the prototype-to-stakeholder-review cycle from days to hours. However, each tool has hard limits: Make prototypes are static, Sites output is not a CMS, AI image expansion fails on complex textures, and all AI features are gated behind paid plans. Knowing which tasks belong to Figma AI vs a Claude agent prevents building workflows that don't exist.

why.p: Figma AI tools (Config 2025+) compress the prototype-to-stakeholder-review cycle from days to hours. However, each tool has hard limits: Make prototypes are static, Sites output is not a CMS, AI image expansion fails on complex textures, and all AI features are gated behind paid plans. Knowing which tasks belong to Figma AI vs a Claude agent prevents building workflows that don't exist.

list.item: Generating web app prototypes rapidly from a text brief using Figma Make
Removing objects or expanding image backgrounds on the Figma canvas without export/re-import
Publishing a static site from Figma via Figma Sites for early stakeholder review
Producing vector sketch assets using Figma Draw for illustration-heavy UI components
Auditing a team's Figma AI feature adoption and identifying workflow gaps
Production web development — Figma Sites output is for demos and landing pages, not complex apps
Complex multi-state interactive prototyping requiring custom conditional logic
Brand-critical image editing where exact pixel control matters — AI image tools are probabilistic
Figma Draw for precise icon creation — generative output lacks manual vector precision

section.name: figma-make
section.p: Generates web app prototypes from text prompts with collaborative editing and design system token integration.

list.item: Generates web app prototypes from text prompts; collaborative editing with AI assistant
Direct connection to design system tokens (partial — not all variables are respected)
Limit: static placeholders only; no real data source connection
Limit: conditional logic is limited; not suitable for complex multi-state interactive prototyping
Limit: browser-only; no public API as of 2026

rule.statement: Use Figma Make for 10-minute prototype generation; always rebuild final UI in proper Figma components.
rule.rationale: Make output is for exploration and stakeholder alignment, not as a design deliverable or production starting point.

rule.statement: Keep Figma Make prompts under 150 words. The in-app AI context window is limited; longer prompts produce degraded output.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated Figma Make prompts often exceed the effective context window; brevity and specificity improve output quality.

section.name: figma-draw
section.p: Generative AI sketching for vector graphics assistance.

list.item: Generative AI sketching for vector graphics assistance
Limit: generative output lacks precision of manual vector work; not suitable for production icon sets
Limit: no headless API; in-canvas generative sketching only

rule.statement: Keep Figma Draw sketches in a separate draft page. Do not mix generative vector sketches with component library frames.
rule.rationale: Mixing generative and production content in the same Figma pages creates ambiguity during handoff and audits.

section.name: figma-sites
section.p: Publishing static sites directly from Figma for stakeholder previews.

list.item: Publishes static site directly from Figma for stakeholder previews
Limit: not a CMS; content changes require re-export from Figma
Limit: no server-side rendering; poor SEO for SEO-sensitive use cases

rule.statement: Publish Figma Sites only on a custom subdomain with a clear "prototype, not production" header.
rule.rationale: Stakeholders who receive a Figma Sites URL without context may treat it as the production site; explicit labeling prevents scope confusion.

section.name: ai-image-tools
section.p: Canvas-native image editing tools: Erase Objects, Isolate Objects, Expand Images.

list.item: Erase Objects — select and remove unwanted elements from canvas images
Isolate Objects — separate subjects for moving or applying effects
Expand Images — extend backgrounds for new formats
Advantage: works directly on canvas without export/re-import cycle
Limit: probabilistic; fails on complex textures, faces, and branded photography

rule.statement: Generate 3 variations when using AI image Expand for hero banners; single-run outputs are often asymmetric.
rule.rationale: AI expansion is probabilistic; single-run outputs frequently have visible seams or asymmetric fill. Selection from multiple variants is the reliable pattern.

section.name: plan-requirements
section.p: Gating and access control for Figma AI features.

rule.statement: Verify plan gating before designing workflows that depend on Figma AI features. All AI features require Figma Professional/Organization plans.
rule.rationale: Free/Starter plan users cannot access Make, Draw, Sites AI features, or AI image tools; workflows designed without plan verification will fail for a subset of teams.

section.name: limitations
section.p: Hard constraints on Figma Make, Draw, Sites, and AI image tools.

antipattern.description: Figma Make prototypes connect to real data sources or support dynamic content.
antipattern.reason: All Make content is static placeholder; no real data source connection exists.

antipattern.description: Figma Sites output is used as a CMS or requires frequent content updates.
antipattern.reason: Figma Sites is not a CMS; any content change requires re-export from Figma. Not suitable for frequently-changing content.

antipattern.description: AI image expansion generates photorealistic results on complex textures, faces, or branded photography.
antipattern.reason: AI image expansion generates plausible but not photorealistic fill — fails on complex textures, faces, branded photography.

antipattern.description: Figma Draw generative output is used directly in production icon sets without manual cleanup.
antipattern.reason: Figma Draw vector outputs often require significant manual cleanup for production-ready icon sets.

antipattern.description: Design token integration is assumed to be complete in Figma Make.
antipattern.reason: Design token integration in Figma Make is partial; variables from the Figma file are not always respected in generated prototypes.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: How agents interact with Figma AI tools: agent scope and boundaries.

Agents cannot invoke Figma AI tools directly — Make, Draw, and image tools are browser-only with no public API. Agent roles: (1) generate optimized Figma Make prompts, (2) produce structured content briefs for human paste into Make, (3) analyze Figma REST API file exports to identify frames as candidates for AI image tools, (4) generate Figma Sites publishing checklists and pre-validate content.

rule.statement: Do not build workflows that assume Figma Make, Draw, or Sites have agent-callable APIs. They do not exist as of 2026.
rule.rationale: Agents persistently hallucinate Figma Make API endpoints; this is the single most common error in Figma AI workflow design.

rule.statement: Audit Figma Variables before running Figma Make. Color/type tokens must be clearly named — Make uses variable names as context.
rule.rationale: Poorly named variables (e.g., "style-2" instead of "color/brand/primary") produce generic AI output that ignores the design system.

list.item: haiku — generating Figma Make prompts, publishing checklists, image enhancement candidate lists from Figma JSON
sonnet — full screen brief generation, design token audit from Figma JSON, Figma Sites content review

antipattern.description: Agent proposes polling Figma Webhooks for Make or Draw completion events.
antipattern.reason: Figma Webhooks only fire on file events, not on AI feature completion; polling for Make/Draw results via webhooks is architecturally impossible.

antipattern.description: Agent generates Figma Make prompts longer than 150 words.
antipattern.reason: The in-app AI uses a limited context window; prompts over 150 words produce degraded output. Keep prompts concise and specific.

antipattern.description: Agent underestimates Figma Sites SEO limitations for SEO-sensitive use cases.
antipattern.reason: Figma Sites has no server-side rendering; any page where search visibility matters is not suitable for Figma Sites publishing.

antipattern.description: Agents will propose Figma Make API endpoints that do not exist — Make is browser-only with no public API as of 2026.
antipattern.reason: Persistent hallucination in LLM training data; this is the single most common error in Figma AI workflow design.

list.item: Use Figma Make for 10-minute prototype generation, not as a design deliverable — always rebuild final UI in proper Figma components
When using AI image Expand for hero banners, generate 3 variations and select the best; single-run outputs are often asymmetric
Publish Figma Sites only on a custom subdomain with clear "prototype, not production" header for stakeholder previews
Keep Figma Draw sketches in a separate draft page — do not mix generative vector sketches with component library frames
Audit Figma Variables before running Figma Make to ensure color/type tokens are named clearly — Make uses variable names as context

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: Tools and services for Figma AI integration and analysis.

list.item: figma-rest-api (JS) — Read file structure, export frames, inspect variables (https://www.figma.com/developers/api)
figma-js — Node.js wrapper for Figma REST API (npm i figma-js)
figma-export — CLI batch export of Figma frames by node name (https://github.com/lucasecdb/figma-export)

section.p: Services: Figma Make (SaaS, no direct API, browser-only), Figma Draw (SaaS, no API, in-canvas), Figma Sites (SaaS, no publish API), Figma REST API v1 (SaaS, read files/components/variables, no write for AI features), Figma Webhooks (SaaS, event triggers on file change/publish), Adobe Firefly API (SaaS, alternative for image generation tasks).

example.title: Figma Make Prompt Generation
example.p: Generate a Figma Make prompt for: [screen description, e.g., "SaaS dashboard — weekly analytics overview, 3 KPI cards, line chart, recent activity list"]. Design system context: [Figma file has: color tokens blue-600, gray-100; typography: Inter; component library: Cards, Charts]. Output: one concise Figma Make-ready prompt (max 150 words) optimized for design system adherence.

example.title: Figma Sites Review
example.p: Review this Figma Sites page specification for: (1) missing meta tags for SEO, (2) content that requires CMS update capability (replace with static alternatives), (3) interactive elements not supported in static Figma Sites export. Input spec: [paste page outline].

template.purpose: Figma REST API script to find raster-fill nodes suitable for AI image tools
Prompt for generating optimized Figma Make input prompts
Prompt for reviewing Figma Sites page specs for SEO and static limitations
